meta.description: mostlyCBD Evidence Library — curated cannabis research. Scored, tagged, and human-reviewed before publish.

studies.title: CBD: A Systematic Review of Clinical and Preclinical Evidence in the Treatment of Pain
studies.authors: Multiple authors, MDPI Pharmaceuticals
studies.plain_summary: PRISMA-guided review of studies on CBD for pain. Found consistent analgesic properties across neuropathic, inflammatory, and chronic pain with no significant adverse effects reported in clinical or preclinical trials. Notes activation of HT-A receptors as a likely mechanism.
studies.score_notes: Systematic review following PRISMA, peer-reviewed, recent (2024), broad scope, no conflicts reported

studies.title: Are Cannabis-Based Medicines a Useful Treatment for Neuropathic Pain? A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Almuntashiri N, El Sharazly BM, Carter WG
studies.plain_summary: Reviewed RCTs from - on cannabis-based medicines for neuropathic pain. Found promising evidence for balanced THC:CBD formulations (nabiximols/Sativex), with low risk of bias in most studies. Notes regulatory barriers as a major obstacle to expanded research.
studies.score_notes: Fresh (2025), systematic review of RCTs, dual risk-of-bias review, peer-reviewed journal, no conflicts

studies.title: Cannabis-Based Products for Chronic Pain: An Updated Systematic Review
studies.authors: Chou R, Fu R, Ahmed AY, Morasco BJ
studies.plain_summary: Comparable THC:CBD ratio products may slightly reduce pain severity with small effect sizes. High-THC-only products showed increased adverse events.
studies.score_notes: Annals of Internal Medicine (top-tier), AHRQ-funded (no industry bias), 2026 publication, PROSPERO registered, dual extraction

studies.title: Effectiveness of Cannabidiol to Manage Chronic Pain: A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Multiple authors, PRISMA 2020
studies.journal: Journal of Pain & Palliative Care Pharmacotherapy
studies.plain_summary: Majority showed pain reduction of 42 - 66% with CBD. Three studies showed no significant improvement. Concludes CBD may be useful for chronic pain but calls for more robust designs and standardized dosing protocols.
studies.score_notes: PRISMA-guided, multi-database, no conflicts, moderate heterogeneity across included studies

studies.title: Therapeutic Potential of Cannabidiol (CBD) in Anxiety Disorders: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
studies.authors: Multiple authors
studies.journal: European Journal of Medicinal Chemistry Reports
studies.plain_summary: This meta-analysis revealed a substantial significant impact of CBD on anxiety with a considerable effect size. In addition, this meta-analysis focuses on the efficacy of CBD in treating anxiety disorders such as generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), social anxiety disorder (SAD), and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). However, caution should be exercised in interpreting our findings due to the limited size of the clinical sample, and additional trials ought to be carried out if deemed necessary.
studies.score_notes: Meta-analysis with effect size calculation, recent (2024), peer-reviewed, notes limitations honestly

studies.title: Evidence for Use of Cannabinoids in Mood Disorders, Anxiety Disorders, and PTSD: A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Stanciu CN, Brunette MF, Teja N, Budney AJ
studies.plain_summary: In this systematic review, the authors summarize the design and results of controlled trials assessing the effects of THC and CBD on affective disorders, anxiety disorders, and posttraumatic stress disorder (PTSD).
studies.score_notes: Dartmouth, Psychiatric Services (peer-reviewed, clinical), honest negative finding, no conflicts

studies.title: Cannabidiol in Anxiety and Sleep: A Large Case Series
studies.authors: Shannon S, Lewis N, Lee H, Hughes S
studies.plain_summary: A large retrospective case series at a psychiatric clinic involving clinical application of CBD for anxiety and sleep complaints as an adjunct to usual treatment. The retrospective chart review included monthly documentation of anxiety and sleep quality in 103 adult patients.
studies.score_notes: Real-world clinical data, peer-reviewed, honest about limitations; docked for lack of control group

studies.title: Cannabidiol as a Potential Treatment for Anxiety Disorders
studies.authors: Blessing EM, Steenkamp MM, Manzanares J, Marmar CR
studies.plain_summary: Foundational review of preclinical and clinical evidence. Demonstrates CBD's efficacy across GAD, panic disorder, SAD, OCD, and PTSD in animal models. Human data support anxiolytic effects with no anxiogenic properties and excellent safety profile.
studies.score_notes: Neurotherapeutics (high-impact), foundational paper, NYU authors, docked slightly for age (2015) and limited chronic dosing data

studies.title: Clinical and Cognitive Improvement Following Full-Spectrum, High-CBD Treatment for Anxiety: Open-Label Phase 2 Trial
studies.authors: Dahlgren MK et al.
studies.plain_summary: Outpatients with moderate-to-severe anxiety treated with weeks of full-spectrum high-CBD sublingual solution. Significant anxiety reduction on primary outcomes. Improvements also in mood, sleep, quality of life, and executive function.
studies.score_notes: Nature Portfolio journal, full-spectrum (real-world relevant), docked for small n=14 and open-label design

studies.title: Cannabis, Pain, and Sleep: Lessons from Therapeutic Clinical Trials of Sativex
studies.authors: Russo EB
studies.plain_summary: Reviews Phase I–III Sativex (1:1 THC:CBD) trials across 2,000 subjects with 1,000 patient-years exposure. Marked improvement in subjective sleep in patients with neuropathic pain, MS, cancer pain, and rheumatoid arthritis. CBD has mild activating effect; THC-dominant extracts show residual sedation.
studies.score_notes: Ethan Russo (respected cannabinoid researcher), massive clinical dataset, docked for age (2007) and Sativex industry connection

studies.title: The Interplay between the Endocannabinoid System, Epilepsy, and Cannabinoids
studies.authors: Cheung KAK, Peiris H, Wallace G, Holland OJ, Mitchell MD
studies.plain_summary: Examines how the ECS regulates neuroinflammation and seizure activity, and how cannabinoids may reduce incidence and severity of epileptic events. CBD highlighted as most promising therapeutic cannabinoid due to non-psychoactive profile and neuroprotective properties. Notes lack of rigorous long-term safety data.
studies.score_notes: Open access, clear mechanism review, peer-reviewed MDPI, docked for age and review-only design

studies.title: Targeting Endocannabinoid System in Epilepsy: For Good or for Bad
studies.authors: MartÃ­nez-Aguirre C, Cinar R, Rocha L — Center for Research and Advanced Studies, Mexico City
studies.plain_summary: Presents a nuanced view: ECS activation can both suppress AND facilitate seizure activity depending on conditions. CB signaling can promote neuroinflammation under certain pathological conditions. Important counterbalance to purely positive framings of cannabinoid use in epilepsy.
studies.score_notes: Elsevier Neuroscience, honest mixed finding, important counterpoint, no conflicts

studies.title: Oral Cannabis for Taxane-Induced Neuropathy: A Pilot Randomized Placebo-Controlled Study
studies.authors: Haney M, Choo T, Tiersten A et al. — Columbia University
studies.plain_summary: 8-week double-blind RCT in breast cancer patients with taxane-induced neuropathy. Both groups improved over time, but cannabis patients had significantly higher neuropathy ratings and lower functional well-being in final weeks vs placebo. A carefully conducted negative trial — rare and valuable.
studies.score_notes: Columbia University, double-blind RCT design, 2025, rigorous — negative results are essential to include

studies.title: Cannabidiol: A Potential New Alternative for the Treatment of Anxiety, Depression, and Psychotic Disorders
studies.authors: GarcÃ­a-GutiÃ©rrez MS et al.
studies.plain_summary: Reviews animal and human data for CBD's anxiolytic, antidepressant, and antipsychotic potential. Key targets include CB/CB receptors, -HTA receptors, and neurogenesis factors. Preliminary clinical trials show positive risk-benefit profile.
studies.score_notes: Frontiers in Psychiatry, open access, honest about evidence gaps, no conflicts

studies.title: ALS, the Endocannabinoid System, and Exogenous Cannabinoids: Current State and Clinical Implications
studies.authors: Denton TT, Carter GT, Goddard M et al. — Washington State University
studies.plain_summary: Reviews preclinical and emerging clinical evidence for cannabinoids in ALS. THC, CBD, CBG, and other cannabinoids show antioxidative, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties in SOD mouse models, including delayed disease onset and prolonged neuronal survival.
studies.score_notes: WSU/Steve Gleason Institute, Muscle & Nerve journal (respected), 2025, Ethan Russo co-author

studies.title: Cannabidiol Modulates Serotonergic Transmission and Reverses Both Allodynia and Anxiety-like Behavior in Neuropathic Pain
studies.authors: De Gregorio D, McLaughlin RJ, Posa L et al.
studies.plain_summary: Preclinical study demonstrating CBD reverses allodynia (pain from non-painful stimuli) and anxiety in a neuropathic pain model via -HTA serotonin receptor activation. Directly relevant to patients who experience co-occurring neuropathic pain and anxiety — suggests a unified mechanism.
studies.score_notes: Published in PAIN (top-tier pain journal), important mechanism discovery, docked for preclinical-only design

studies.title: Oral capsules of tetra-hydro-cannabinol (THC), cannabidiol (CBD) and their combination in peripheral neuropathic pain treatment
studies.authors: Zubcevic K, Petersen M, Bach FW et al.
studies.plain_summary: Randomized, double-blind, trial (8 weeks) with treatment arms for cannabidiol (CBD), tetra-hydro-cannabinol (THC), CBD and THC combination (CBD/THC), and placebo in a 1:1:1:1 ratio and flexible drug doses (CBD 5-50 mg, THC 2.5-25 mg, and CBD/THC 5 mg/2.5 mg-50 mg/25 mg).
studies.score_notes: Study type: randomized (+18); Journal: Eur J Pain (+12); Recency: 2023, 3 years old (+10); CBD relevance: 3 keywords (+12); Sample size signals (+8)

studies.title: Cannabis-based medicines for chronic neuropathic pain in adults
studies.authors: Ateş G, Welsch P, Klose P et al.
studies.plain_summary: To assess the benefits and harms of cannabis-based medicines (herbal, plant-based, synthetic) compared to placebo or conventional drugs for chronic neuropathic pain conditions in adults.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+14 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:4hits(+12) | sample(+7)

studies.title: The efficacy and safety of cannabinoids for the treatment of mental disorders and substance use disorders: a systematic review and meta-analysis
studies.authors: Wilson J, Dobson O, Langcake A et al.
studies.plain_summary: Systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials (RCTs) testing the efficacy and safety of cannabinoids as the primary treatment for mental disorders or SUDs.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+15 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:1hits(+3) | sample(+13)

studies.title: Effectiveness of cannabinoids on subjective sleep quality in people with and without insomnia or poor sleep: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised studies
studies.authors: da Silva GHS, Barbosa EC, de Lima FR et al.
studies.plain_summary: Subjective sleep quality in people with and without insomnia or poor sleep: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised studies.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6) | sample(+11)

studies.title: CARE - A Retrospective, Dual-Cohort, 24-Week Real-World Study from the German Pain e-Registry on the Effectiveness and Safety of CBD-Dominant Oral Cannabis Extracts versus THC/Dronabinol in Older Patients with Chronic Pain
studies.authors: Überall MA, Müller-Schwefe PCG, Küster MA et al.
studies.plain_summary: In this real-world cohort of older adults with chronic pain, CBD-Ddominant extracts were associated with more favorable multidimensional outcomes and better tolerability than THC/DRO. Findings are exploratory and warrant confirmation in randomized controlled trials.
studies.score_notes: type:randomized controlled trial [text](+22) | journal:+12 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9) | sample(+7) | caution:-3

studies.title: Medicinal cannabis for symptom control in advanced cancer: a double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomised clinical trial of 1:1 tetrahydrocannabinol and cannabidiol
studies.authors: Hardy JR, Greer RM, Pelecanos AM et al.
studies.plain_summary: Patients can be informed that a 1:1 THC:CBD combination cannabis oil was no better than palliative care alone in palliating symptoms in patients with advanced cancer.
studies.score_notes: type:randomized controlled trial [pubtype](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:4hits(+12) | sample(+5)

studies.title: Randomized and Blind Evaluation of the Efficacy of a Full-Spectrum Oral Cannabis sativa Oil Extract, Standardized Based on CBD-A, CBD and THC-A, THC in Canines with Chronic Osteoarthritis
studies.authors: Benjamin ET, Maria Teresa SE, Alexander NS et al.
studies.plain_summary: This study shows that Cannabis sativa oil extract can reduce pain in dogs with chronic osteoarthritis. Conventional treatments may cause adverse effects or lose effectiveness over time, but this research suggests that Cannabis therapy is a safe and effective adjunct.
studies.score_notes: type:double-blind [text](+20) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:6hits(+18)

studies.title: Impact of Medical Cannabis on the Quality of Life of Cancer Patients: A Critical Review
studies.authors: Correa LG, Tucci AM
studies.plain_summary: The studies suggest that medical cannabis can improve mental health, sleep, appetite, and pain in cancer patients and decrease nausea, vomiting, and the use of other medications, such as opioids. Increased survival time and cognitive function improvements were also observed, with mild or moderate adverse effects.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [text](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6) | sample(+7)

studies.title: Modulating the endocannabinoid system in alcohol use disorder: A translational systematic review and meta-analysis of preclinical and human studies
studies.authors: Costa GPA, Cerezo-Matias MA, Funaro MC et al.
studies.plain_summary: Preclinical evidence supports ECS modulation, particularly CB-1R antagonism and CBD, as promising strategies for reducing alcohol use behaviors, clinical translation has been limited by safety concerns, methodological inconsistencies, and under-investigation of novel compounds.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [text](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:4hits(+12)

studies.title: Cannabinoids for Medical Purposes in Children: A Living Systematic Review
studies.authors: Chhabra M, Paul A, Abulannaz O et al.
studies.plain_summary: Cannabinoids are used for medical purposes in children with an expanding array of products and conditions studied, including drug-resistant epilepsy, autism spectrum disorder, symptoms experienced by patients with cancer, and various other health conditions.
studies.score_notes: type:systematic review [pubtype](+23) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:4hits(+12) | sample(+5) | caution:-3

studies.title: Therapeutically Motivated Cannabis Use for Anxiety: Daily and Longitudinal Reductions Vary Between Flower and Edible Products
studies.authors: Rosa L, Lisano JK, Skrzynski CJ et al.
studies.journal: Int J Environ Res Public Health
studies.plain_summary: This study provides within-person, naturalistic findings that cannabis use is associated with short-term reductions in anxiety, with CBD products producing the most consistent benefits. The results suggest that, among individuals who already choose to use cannabis for anxiety, CBD-dominant products were associated with more consistent reductions in self-reported anxiety, although further placebo-controlled trials are needed.
studies.score_notes: type:randomized controlled trial [pubtype](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6) | sample(+7)

studies.title: Efficacy and Safety of Cannabinoids for Autism Spectrum Disorder: An Updated Systematic Review
studies.authors: Pereira DA, Cheidde L, Megiolaro MDR et al.
studies.plain_summary: This systematic review assessed the potential impact of cannabinoids on ASD symptoms and adverse effects. While preliminary evidence suggests potential benefits, particularly for anxiety, sleep, and behavior, the findings remain inconclusive due to study heterogeneity and methodological limitations.
studies.score_notes: type:systematic review [text](+23) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6) | sample(+7)

studies.title: The differential effects of medicinal cannabis on mental health: A systematic review
studies.authors: de Bode N, Kroon E, Sznitman SR et al.
studies.plain_summary: Overall, the evidence suggests both positive and negative effects on mental health symptoms. More specifically, the most consistent improvements were observed in anxiety and sleep-related symptoms, as well as less intense withdrawal and craving in SUDs.
studies.score_notes: type:systematic review [pubtype](+23) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6) | sample(+7)

studies.title: Cannabinoids versus placebo for pain: A systematic review with meta-analysis and Trial Sequential Analysis
studies.authors: Barakji J, Korang SK, Feinberg J et al.
studies.plain_summary: Cannabinoids reduced chronic pain and improved quality of sleep, but the effect sizes are of questionable importance. Cannabinoids had no effects on acute pain or cancer pain and increased the risks of non-serious adverse events.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2023(+10) | sample(+15)

studies.title: A systematic review of highly purified cannabidiol in developmental and epileptic encephalopathies and complex treatment-resistant epilepsies: Changes in seizure frequency and adverse events
studies.authors: Coppola A, Moore-Ramdin L, Navetta M et al.
studies.plain_summary: Summarizes evidence that CBD, particularly Epidiolex/Epidyolex, may have the potential to reduce seizures in patients with a range of developmental and epileptic encephalopathies and chronic treatment-resistant epilepsies. While adverse effects were common across studies, they were usually mild or moderate. More research is needed to confirm these initial findings. Notably, the studies identified by this review were consistent in showing the benefits with CBD, regardless of different patient populations.
studies.score_notes: type:systematic review [pubtype](+23) | journal:+10 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9) | sample(+1)

studies.title: The Potential Use of Cannabidiol in the Treatment of Opioid Use Disorder: A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Shafie M, Ing K, Rostam-Abadi Y et al.
studies.plain_summary: This systematic review highlights the potential of CBD as a beneficial treatment option for addressing cravings and anxiety symptoms during abstinence in individuals with opioid use disorder, based on findings from human studies.
studies.score_notes: type:systematic review [pubtype](+23) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9) | sample(+3)

studies.title: Randomised Controlled Trial Evidence on Medicinal Cannabis for Treatment of Mental Health and Substance Use Disorders: A Scoping Review
studies.authors: Cooling S, Bonomo YA, Castle D et al.
studies.plain_summary: Overall, whilst there may be some potential therapeutic benefits for medicinal cannabis in certain psychiatric disorders, the findings of the present review reiterate the need for more high-quality, adequately powered RCTs investigating the use of medicinal cannabis for mental health conditions.
studies.score_notes: type:randomised controlled trial [text](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:4hits(+12)

studies.title: Regular cannabinoid use and inflammatory biomarkers: Systematic review and hierarchical meta-analysis
studies.authors: Belvederi Murri M, Guglielmo R, Zizzi A et al.
studies.plain_summary: Cannabinoid use is associated with concurrent pro- and anti-inflammatory modulation in non-medical populations, consistent with immunomodulatory effects rather than a uniform pro- or anti-inflammatory shift.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [text](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:1hits(+3) | sample(+5)

studies.title: The Use of Cannabinoids in the Treatment of Peripheral Neuropathy and Neuropathic Pain: A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Choi J, Li G, Stephens KL et al.
studies.plain_summary: Our analysis of the literature shows that cannabis-based medicines may be effective in treating the pain and symptoms of peripheral neuropathy. These findings suggest the applicability of cannabis-based medicines for peripheral neuropathy.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [text](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | sample(+9)

studies.title: Synergistic Neuroimmune Modulation by Cannabidiol and Probiotics for Therapeutic Advancement in CNS Disorders: A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Baghel Chauhan S, Jaitawat DPS, Jain C et al.
studies.plain_summary: The observed benefits of CBD and probiotics suggest a synergistic mechanism through gut-brain axis modulation, addressing both neurological and immunological pathways. While animal studies are promising, translation to clinical practice requires further evidence.
studies.score_notes: type:systematic review [text](+23) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9)

studies.title: UK Medical Cannabis Registry: a case series analysing clinical outcomes of medicinal cannabis therapy for fibromyalgia
studies.authors: Varadpande M, Erridge S, Aggarwal A et al.
studies.plain_summary: This study found that cannabis-based medical products were associated with short to medium-term improvements in pain, anxiety, sleep, and general quality-of-life in patients with fibromyalgia.
studies.score_notes: type:randomised controlled trial [text](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:1hits(+3) | sample(+7)

studies.title: Cannabis for medical use versus opioids for chronic non-cancer pain: a systematic review and network meta-analysis of randomised clinical trials
studies.authors: Jeddi HM, Busse JW, Sadeghirad B et al.
studies.plain_summary: Cannabis for medical use may be similarly effective and result in fewer discontinuations than opioids for chronic non-cancer pain.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+14 | year:2024(+12) | cbd_relevance:1hits(+3) | sample(+1)

studies.title: Cannabinoids for Acute Postoperative Pain Management: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Clinical Trials
studies.authors: González Cárdenas VH, Valdivieso Díaz M, Mateus Almeciga CF et al.
studies.plain_summary: The management of severe pain after surgery, and the use of intravenous drugs with limited and potentially unsafe effectiveness, has led to the exploration of new treatment options to minimize side effects while improving pain relief. Cannabinoids have been suggested as a potential solution for managing post-surgical pain, but there is currently insufficient evidence to assess their effectiveness.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+12 | year:2025(+14) | sample(+3)

studies.title: Exploring the neuroprotective effects and underlying mechanisms of medical cannabinoids in ischemic stroke: a systematic meta-analysis with bibliometric mapping of cerebral ischemia research
studies.authors: Li X, Wen L, Du Y et al.
studies.plain_summary: Medical cannabinoids exert multi-target neuroprotection in ischemic stroke by improving cerebral blood flow, reducing brain edema and BBB permeability, and inhibiting oxidative stress, neuroinflammation, apoptosis, and excitotoxicity. Future research should focus on high-quality clinical trials to validate these findings and translate medical cannabinoids into clinical practice.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [text](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6) | sample(+1)

studies.title: Evaluation of the efficacy, safety, and pharmacokinetics of nanodispersible cannabidiol oral solution (150 mg/mL) versus placebo in mild to moderate anxiety subjects: A double blind multicenter randomized clinical trial
studies.authors: Gundugurti PR, Banda N, Yadlapalli SSR et al.
studies.plain_summary: Nanodispersible CBD was therapeutically safe with no serious adverse events, well tolerated, and effective for the treatment of mild to moderate anxiety disorders, as well as associated depression and sleep quality disturbances.
studies.score_notes: type:randomized controlled trial [pubtype](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2024(+12) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9) | sample(+3)

studies.title: Effectiveness of Full Spectrum Cannabis Extracts in the Treatment of Chronic Pain: An Open Label Study
studies.authors: F A, M L, G B et al.
studies.plain_summary: Of 88 patients, 51 individuals reported a decrease in pain, 38 a decrease in anxiety and 48 in insomnia, with 'decrease' defined as symptom reduction of 50% or more between the first and last consultation. In addition, 23 subjects reduced or discontinued other analgesics and/or anti-inflammatory drugs during the trial. Adverse effects were mild and reversible.
studies.score_notes: type:clinical trial [pubtype](+17) | journal:+12 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9) | sample(+1)

studies.title: Effects of Different Cannabinoid Formulations on Anxiety-Related Disorders, and Tourette Syndrome: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
studies.authors: Raminelli AO, Simei JLQ, Guimarães FS et al.
studies.plain_summary: The anxiolytic effects of CBD may also be mediated by its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective properties, which contribute to reducing neural oxidative stress and neuroinflammation.77 These mechanisms could play a role in modulating anxiety-related pathways and improving overall brain resilience.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6)

studies.title: Therapeutic Potential for Cannabidiol on Alzheimer's Disease-Related Neuroinflammation: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
studies.authors: Wu S, Rajiah T, Ali AB
studies.plain_summary: CBD remains a biologically plausible, multi-pathway candidate for modulating neuroinflammation and behaviorally relevant circuits in Alzheimer's Disease. Preclinical signals are broadly convergent; clinical signals, especially for agitation and caregiver burden, are promising yet heterogeneous, reflecting population complexity and design variation rather than an absence of pharmacological activity.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6)

studies.title: The Interplay Between CB(2) and NMDA Receptors in Parkinson's Disease
studies.authors: Reyes-Resina I, Lillo J, Raïch I et al.
studies.plain_summary: Cannabinoid action on CB receptor 2 could prevent neuronal death and promote polarization of microglia to the neuroprotective M2 phenotype. Indeed, the endocannabinoids, 2-AG and anandamide, have antiparkinsonian properties; 2-AG is neuroprotective, and anandamide provides symptomatic relief. Furthermore, cannabinoids have shown to be effective in decreasing axonal transport of α-syn fibrils. Thus, the potential of cannabinoids for the treatment of Parkinson's disease arises as a promising new pathway that should be further explored
studies.score_notes: type:journal article [pubtype](+8) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14)

studies.title: Effects of Cannabidiol on Social Relating, Anxiety, and Parental Stress in Autistic Children: A Randomized Controlled Crossover Trial
studies.authors: Parrella NF, Hill AT, Enticott PG et al.
studies.plain_summary: While no significant changes were seen in overall social responsiveness, CBD led to improvements in specific social behaviors, reduced anxiety, and lowered parental stress. These findings suggest potential benefits of CBD for some autism‐related symptoms, but larger studies are needed to confirm effectiveness.
studies.score_notes: type:randomized controlled trial [pubtype](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9)

studies.title: Cannabis and cannabidiol for postoperative pain management in orthopedic surgery: a scoping review
studies.authors: Tran K, Odland K, Polly DW Jr
studies.plain_summary: Whereas CBD-only interventions showed mixed results, THC/CBD combinations demonstrated modest potential for opioid-sparing effects, with neutral safety profiles. One THC-only study reported increased opioid use and length of stay, though confounding variables were present.
studies.score_notes: type:clinical trial [text](+17) | journal:+12 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9)

studies.title: The Pleiotropic Influence of Cannabidiol and Tetrahydrocannabinol on Inflammatory Biomarkers: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analytical Synthesis
studies.authors: Candeloro BM, de Oliveira CM, Gimenez FVM et al.
studies.plain_summary: Overall, preclinical and early clinical data converge on plausible immunomodulatory mechanisms for phytocannabinoids—particularly the inhibition of the NLRP3 inflammasome and suppression of NF-κB. Yet, clinical translation to consistent systemic biomarker changes is restricted by route, formulation, exposure, and population selection. Well-powered, PK-informed RCTs in selected clinical populations are mandatory before phytocannabinoids can be suggested as systemic anti-inflammatory agents.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6)

studies.title: Therapeutic potential of cannabidiol in oral disorders: A systematic review of clinical evidence
studies.authors: de Abreu LM, Biancardi MR, Di Benedetto M et al.
studies.plain_summary: Topical and intraoral CBD formulations had beneficial effects in reducing pain, muscle tension, gingival inflammation, bacterial load, and aphthous ulcer symptoms. No serious adverse effects were reported. Heterogeneity in dosage, formulation, follow-up duration, and outcome measures limited direct comparison across studies and precluded meta-analysis.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [text](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6)

studies.title: Clinical Outcomes and Patient Profiles in the UK Medical Cannabis Registry: A k-Means Clustering Analysis
studies.authors: Erridge S, Clarke E, McLachlan K et al.
studies.plain_summary: These findings contribute valuable insights to the evolving evidence base for cannabis-based medical products (CBMPs), whilst highlighting the urgent need for high-quality randomized controlled trials or novel methods of evidence evaluation in cannabis-naïve populations to establish definitive therapeutic efficacy across the heterogeneous patient populations currently accessing CBMPs in clinical practice.
studies.score_notes: type:cohort study [text](+12) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:1hits(+3) | sample(+15)

studies.title: Prenatal cannabis exposure and the risk for neuropsychiatric anomalies in the offspring: a systematic review and meta-analysis
studies.authors: Bassalov H, Yakirevich-Amir N, Reuveni I et al.
studies.plain_summary: Prenatal cannabis exposure is not associated with an increased risk of ASD, psychotic symptoms, anxiety, or depression in offspring. However, it may slightly elevate the risk of ADHD and predispose offspring to cannabis consumption. Despite these findings, caution is warranted regarding cannabis use during pregnancy. Further research is imperative, especially given the increasing potency of cannabis in recent years.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2024(+12) | sample(+7)

studies.title: Efficacy and safety of cannabidiol oil in psoriasis: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial
studies.authors: Roongpisuthipong W, Klangjareonchai T, Kurathong S et al.
studies.plain_summary: Cannabidiol oil was well-tolerated; however, it did not result in a significant reduction in psoriasis severity. Temporary improvements in itch relief and sleep onset indicate that further research with higher doses and extended durations is warranted.
studies.score_notes: type:randomized controlled trial [pubtype](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6) | sample(+1)

studies.title: Cross-sectional comparison of cannabis use in adults with neuropathic versus non-neuropathic pain
studies.authors: Laroya CJP, Smith CL, Bindler RJ et al.
studies.plain_summary: Adults whose most bothersome pain condition(s) were neuropathic used cannabis, especially THC and THC/CBD combination products, more frequently than those whose most bothersome pain was non-neuropathic. Participants with neuropathic pain also reported higher levels of pain intensity and interference. Further longitudinal research is needed to confirm whether increased use of THC-rich cannabis provides symptom relief for adults with neuropathic pain.
studies.score_notes: type:cross-sectional [text](+9) | journal:+12 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:4hits(+12) | sample(+5)

studies.title: UK Medical Cannabis Registry: A clinical outcomes analysis for insomnia
studies.authors: Aggarwal A, Erridge S, Cowley I et al.
studies.plain_summary: The findings indicate a promising association between cannabis-based medicinal product treatment and improvements in sleep-specific outcomes and general HRQoL measures. However, it is crucial to acknowledge that these findings must be interpreted with consideration of the limitations in the study’s design, and there is a need for high quality RCTs to assess the long-term efficacy and safety of cannabis-based medicinal product for primary insomnia.
studies.score_notes: type:randomised controlled trial [text](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:1hits(+3) | sample(+5)

studies.title: Effects of cannabidiol in alcohol use disorder patients with and without co-occurring post-traumatic stress disorder: Tolerability but no evidence for efficacy in two randomized proof-of-concept trials
studies.authors: Bogenschutz MP, Blessing E, Dgheim D et al.
studies.plain_summary: These findings support the feasibility and tolerability of twice-daily oral CBD up to 1200 mg/day in actively drinking individuals but do not demonstrate efficacy at the CBD levels that were achieved in this study. Further dose finding and larger, well-powered trials are needed to clarify CBD's therapeutic potential in AUD and comorbid PTSD.
studies.score_notes: type:randomized controlled trial [pubtype](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6) | sample(+1)

studies.title: Unprescribed cannabinoids and multiple sclerosis: a multicenter, cross-sectional, epidemiological study in Lombardy, Italy
studies.authors: Giossi R, Mercenari M, Filippi M et al.
studies.plain_summary: Unprescribed cannabis use is common in patients with MS in Italy, with observed prevalence seemingly superior to the general population, often intended for medical use and without the disclosure to the treating physician, although with potential clinical benefits.
studies.score_notes: type:clinical trial [text](+17) | journal:+8 | year:2024(+12) | sample(+15)

studies.title: Safety and Tolerability of Low-Dose Full-Spectrum Cannabidiol in Long-Term Virally Suppressed Adults with HIV: A Randomized Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Trial
studies.authors: Clemence Couton, Mathilde Wanneveich, Barbara De Dieuleveult et al.
studies.plain_summary: Abstract Introduction People with long-term virologically suppressed HIV (PWH) experience chronic inflammation. Beneficial effects such as lower levels of inflammation were reported for cannabis-based medicine but data on the safety of standardized low-dose full-spectrum cannabidiol (CBD) are limited. Methods This double-blind randomized placebo-controlled trial ( NCT ) included ART-treated PWH with undetectable viremia (median time on efficient ART years, median age years), encompassing % women.
studies.score_notes: type:double-blind [text](+20) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9)

studies.title: Oral Cannabidiol in the Treatment of Myofascial Pain Disorder of the Temporomandibular Region: A Placebo-Controlled Randomized Clinical Trial
studies.authors: Gwendolyn Reeve, Rachel B. Kutler, Adam Abel et al.
studies.plain_summary: Introduction: The efficacy of oral cannabidiol for management of myofascial pain disorder (MPD) of the temporomandibular region remains understudied. Therefore, the purpose of this study was to determine if cannabinoids provide pain relief and improved jaw function in patients with MPD of the temporomandibular region. Methods: This is a single-center, prospective, double-blind, randomized, placebo-controlled clinical trial.
studies.score_notes: type:double-blind [text](+20) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6) | sample(+3)

studies.title: Assessing the Role of Cannabis in Managing Spasticity in Multiple Sclerosis: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
studies.authors: AlHabil Y, Saadeddin L, Ishkirat H et al.
studies.plain_summary: Cannabis-based therapies are associated with clinically meaningful improvements in MS-related spasticity, particularly over longer durations. Despite the promising findings, high heterogeneity and suspected bias necessitate caution. Further high-quality randomized trials with standardized protocols and comprehensive safety assessments are warranted to validate efficacy and long-term outcomes.
studies.score_notes: type:meta-analysis [pubtype](+25) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:1hits(+3)

studies.title: Cannabidiol blood metabolite levels after cannabidiol treatment are associated with broadband EEG changes and improvements in visuomotor and non-verbal cognitive abilities in boys with autism requiring higher levels of support
studies.authors: Cazares C, Hutton A, Paez G et al.
studies.plain_summary: CBD metabolite levels in blood had a positive association with receptive vocabulary, nonverbal intelligence and visuomotor coordination. Our data suggest that this daily CBD preparation and administration schedule produced mixed effects, with some children showing improvements in cognitive and behavioral abilities while others demonstrated limited changes.
studies.score_notes: type:randomized controlled trial [pubtype](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6)

studies.title: Cannabinoids: Therapeutic Perspectives for Management of Orofacial Pain, Oral Inflammation and Bone Healing-A Systematic Review
studies.authors: Campana MD, de Paolis G, Sammartino G et al.
studies.plain_summary: CBD demonstrated potential in managing orofacial pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting bone healing. However, these conclusions are drawn from a heterogeneous body of evidence, including multiple preclinical studies with high risk of bias and clinical trials with varying outcomes and endpoints. Rigorous, well-powered, and standardized clinical trials are needed to confirm efficacy, determine safety, and guide dosage and formulation strategies.
studies.score_notes: type:systematic review [pubtype](+23) | journal:+8 | year:2025(+14) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6)

studies.title: Exploring the effects of cannabidiol on pain sensitivity using quantitative sensory testing among individuals receiving methadone or buprenorphine for opioid use disorder: an open-label, proof-of-concept study
studies.authors: Costa GPA, Suh R, Sofuoglu M et al.
studies.plain_summary: This pilot study offers preliminary indications that CBD’s impact on pain sensitivity varies by dose and MOUD (medications for opioid use disorder) type, notably with potential benefits at 400 mg for individuals on buprenorphine. Coupled with its demonstrated safety and tolerability, these findings support future larger, placebo-controlled randomized trials to confirm CBD’s therapeutic utility for pain in this population.
studies.score_notes: type:placebo-controlled [text](+19) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:3hits(+9)

studies.title: Vaporized cannabis versus placebo for acute migraine: A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled crossover trial
studies.authors: Schuster NM, Wallace MS, Marcotte TD et al.
studies.plain_summary: In this double‐blind study, people with migraine treated up to 4 migraine attacks, 1 attack was treated with each of 3 vaporized cannabis flower treatments (THC 6%, CBD 11%, and THC 6% + CBD 11%) or placebo cannabis flower without THC or CBD, within the first 4 h of migraine attack onset. Four puffs of cannabis flower containing THC 6% + CBD 11% was superior to placebo at treating migraine attacks, though the study did not examine the long‐term effects of frequent use.
studies.score_notes: type:randomized controlled trial [pubtype](+22) | journal:+8 | year:2026(+15) | cbd_relevance:2hits(+6)
